records.ety: [OE. apostasie, F. apostasie, L. apostasia, fr. Gr. <?/ a standing off from, a defection, fr. <?/ to stand off, revolt; <?/ from + <?/ to stand. See Off and Stand.]

defs: An abandonment of what one has voluntarily professed; a total desertion of departure from one's faith, principles, or party; esp., the renunciation of a religious faith; as, Julian's apostasy from Christianity.
